title: GT Challenge Assessment Item
description: FROZEN SCHEMA v1.0 — Defines the structure of IRT 3PL-calibrated adaptive assessment items for the GT Challenge platform. Used by QA evaluators, item generators, and the scoring pipeline. Do not modify without a version bump and evaluator migration.
$comment: Aligned with: packages/shared-types/src/schemas.ts (ItemRowSchema), packages/irt-engine/src/types.ts (Item interface), packages/shared-types/src/enums.ts (Domain, AgeBand).

id.description: Unique item identifier. Format: {band_code}-{domain_initial}{4-digit number}. Band codes: K2 (ages 5-7), 35 (ages 8-10), 68 (ages 11-14). Domain initials: r=reasoning, m=math, v=verbal, p=pattern_recognition. Examples: K2-r0001, 35-m0042, 68-p0003.

domain.description: Primary cognitive domain. Must match the domain initial in the id field (r/m/v/p). Maps to Domain enum in shared-types.

age_bands.description: Age bands this item is appropriate for. Most items target one band. Items spanning adjacent bands (e.g., ['K-2','3-5']) are permissible for bridging difficulty. The band code in the id must match the primary (first) age band.

difficulty.description: IRT b parameter (item difficulty). The theta value at which a non-guessing test-taker has a 50% probability of a correct response. Negative = easier, positive = harder. Typical ranges by band: K-2 [-2.5, 1.0], 3-5 [-2.0, 2.0], 6-8 [-0.5, 3.0]. See grade-specs.json for anchors.

discrimination.description: IRT a parameter (item discrimination). Controls steepness of the item characteristic curve. Higher values mean the item differentiates more sharply between ability levels near its difficulty. Below 0.5 = too flat, provides negligible Fisher information. Above 2.5 = suspiciously steep, likely overfitting or a flawed item.

guessing.description: IRT c parameter (pseudo-guessing). Lower asymptote of the item characteristic curve — the probability a very-low-ability examinee answers correctly by chance. Typically 1/num_options: 3 options = 0.33, 4 options = 0.25, 5 options = 0.20. Must be consistent with content.options length.

content.description: The presentable content of the item — what the child sees and what the parent reads on the dashboard.

stem.description: The question text presented to the child. Must respect the reading level, vocabulary ceiling, and max stem length for the item's age band (see grade-specs.json). K-2 stems should use Dolch/Fry sight words and stay under 60 characters where possible. 3-5 stems can use grade-level vocabulary up to ~200 characters. 6-8 stems may use academic vocabulary up to 500 characters.

type.description: Question type. Must be valid for the item's domain per domain-taxonomy.json. reasoning: analogy, odd_one_out, number_series, syllogism, matrix. math: computation, word_problem, comparison, number_sense, estimation. verbal: synonym, antonym, inference, comprehension, definition, vocabulary. pattern_recognition: color_pattern, shape_pattern, size_pattern, number_pattern, matrix, sequence_completion.

text.description: Option display text. Each non-correct option (distractor) should target a specific misconception from misconception-library.json. Option text must be at the same reading level as the stem.

image.description: Optional storage path or URL for visual option content. Used for pattern_recognition and spatial items.

audio.description: Optional audio path for pre-reader accessibility (K-2).

options.description: Answer choices. K-2: 3-4 options. 3-5: 3-4 options. 6-8: 4-5 options. The guessing parameter must equal 1/options.length (within 0.02 tolerance). Distractors must target documented misconceptions — no random wrong answers.

correct_index.description: 0-based index into options array for the correct answer. Must satisfy: 0 <= correct_index < options.length. The correct answer should be unambiguously correct — no trick questions, no 'best answer' subjectivity.

explanation.description: Plain-language explanation for the parent dashboard. Must explain: (1) why the correct answer is right, (2) what reasoning the child should have used, and (3) why the most tempting distractor is wrong. Should be understandable by a non-expert adult.

media.description: Optional images or audio files for the stem (e.g., matrix grids, number lines, shape sequences). Referenced by storage path or URL.

audio_stem.description: Audio version of the stem for pre-readers (K-2). Recommended for all K-2 items. The audio should read the stem exactly, with no additional hints or emphasis.

metadata.description: Psychometric and pedagogical metadata. Used by QA evaluators and the parent dashboard.

cognitive_skill.description: The specific cognitive ability this item measures. Must map to a skill listed in domain-taxonomy.json. Examples: 'analogical reasoning', 'working memory', 'mental rotation', 'deductive inference', 'phonemic awareness', 'numerical estimation', 'fluid reasoning', 'crystallized knowledge', 'visual-spatial processing', 'quantitative reasoning'.

misconception_targeted.description: The specific misconception or common error that the primary distractor catches. Must reference a misconception from misconception-library.json. Example: 'Assumes pattern repeats rather than grows', 'Confuses correlation with causation in syllogisms'.

bloom_level.description: Bloom's taxonomy level. remember = recall a fact or definition. understand = explain a concept in own words. apply = use a known procedure in a new context. analyze = decompose, find patterns, evaluate relationships. Gifted-identification items should skew toward 'apply' and 'analyze'. Items at 'remember' level should be rare and used only for floor/warm-up.

option_index.description: 0-based index of the distractor option. Must not equal content.correct_index.

misconception_id.description: References a misconception id from misconception-library.json (e.g., 'r_35_correlational_trap').

reasoning.description: Explanation of why a child holding this misconception would select this distractor.

distractor_rationale.description: Optional but strongly recommended. Detailed rationale linking each distractor to a specific misconception. One entry per distractor option.

source_reference.description: Optional psychometric or educational source reference (e.g., 'CogAT Form 7 Verbal Battery', 'Raven's Progressive Matrices', 'NNAT3 Pattern Completion'). For traceability, not licensing.
